tweet.text: The 💡 unlock for Codex is shifting from giving it pointed tasks, to instead giving it context for higher objectives. Codex can access its own session history, write files, and build skills—and now with Automations has everything it needs to self-improve from your usage.

raw_text.text: The 💡 unlock for Codex is shifting from giving it pointed tasks, to instead giving it context for higher objectives. Codex can access its own session history, write files, and build skills—and now with Automations has everything it needs to self-improve from your usage.

author.name: Michael Schade
author.description: imagine the possibilities @openai. i like learning new things—say hi! 🏳️‍🌈 m@mschade.me

raw_description.text: imagine the possibilities @openai. i like learning new things—say hi! 🏳️‍🌈 m@mschade.me

author.location: NYC, SF, and here and there

quote.text:
What's new in the Codex app

🔀 Built-in worktrees
Enable multiple agents to work without conflicts
• Use isolated worktrees in same repository
• Review clean diffs and provide inline feedback

📋 Plan mode
Type /plan to go back and forth with Codex
• Create thorough plans before you start coding
• Iterate on your approach with the agent

🗣️ Personalities
Choose the interaction style that fits your vibe
• Use the /personality command across all surfaces
• Pick a pragmatic or conversational style

🚀 Skills
Connect the tools you already use and go beyond writing code
• Deploy to @Vercel, fetch from @Figma, manage @linear, and more
• Bundle your workflows into reusable skills

🔄 Automations
Delegate repetitive recurring tasks in the background
• Set up tasks for issue triage, failure reports, and more
• Combines skills and custom instructions to run on a schedule

Available on macOS, with Windows coming soon

To celebrate, Plus, Pro, Business, Enterprise, and Edu users have doubled rate limits across the Codex app, CLI, IDE extension, and cloud

Codex is also included in Free and Go plans for a limited time

http://openai.com/index/introducing-the-codex-app

card.title: Introducing the Codex app
card.description: Introducing the Codex app for macOS—a command center for AI coding and software development with multiple agents, parallel workflows, and long-running tasks.
